content: Diagnosis requires imaging and tissue confirmation. Preferred imaging: pancreatic protocol CT (multiphase with arterial and portal venous phases) or MRI/MRCP; assesses resectability (arterial involvement - celiac, SMA, hepatic artery; venous involvement - SMV, portal vein). Endoscopic ultrasound (EUS) with FNA/FNB provides tissue diagnosis, especially for small lesions or when CT-guided biopsy not feasible. ERCP for biliary stenting if obstructive jaundice. CA 19-9 tumor marker: elevated in 80% but not diagnostic (can be elevated in benign biliary obstruction); useful for monitoring treatment response; Lewis antigen-negative individuals (5-10%) cannot produce CA 19-9. Staging: TNM system; potentially resectable, borderline resectable, locally advanced, metastatic classification guides treatment. Germline testing recommended for all patients (NCCN guidelines) given high rate of actionable mutations. PET-CT may detect occult metastases. Diagnostic laparoscopy considered before major resection to rule out peritoneal metastases not visible on imaging.
